texto: Want to talk about it?" I asked gently.He smirked at me. "I appreciate the offer, but I'm a guy. We don't do that." My nose scrunched up in confusion. "We don't discuss our feelings.""That's a relief; I don't want to talk about it either.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Lani Woodland que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Want to talk about it?" I asked gently.He smirked at me. "I appreciate the offer, but I'm a guy. …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre humor, feelings, about, com foco em humor.

frase_original: Want to talk about it?" I asked gently.He smirked at me. "I appreciate the offer, but I'm a guy. We don't do that." My nose scrunched up in confusion. "We don't discuss our feelings.""That's a relief; I don't want to talk about it either.

texto: That was . . .” I trailed off trying to find the proper adjective.“Long overdue?”“Long overdue? You’re the one who got skittish when I mentioned how I felt and backed away when we almost kissed.”“You call me on all my crap, don’t you?” He laughed throwing his head back. “That’s one of the things I love about you,” he said. His fingersskimmed up my shoulders until they cradled my neck and my whole body tingling.

Y…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, romance, reflexao, com foco em reflexao.
